work_unit: scripts/autonomous-loop.sh run_round temp file leak on SIGINT
situation: audit-2026-07-02-002 finding AS-EC-007 (priority 7): run_round() mktemp at line 98 → claude -p at line 99 → shred at line 106. If SIGINT/SIGTERM arrives between mktemp and shred, global trap (line 26) fires `kill 0; exit 130`, skipping shred → temp file with claude output (sensitive content) leaked to /tmp.
action: Wrap mktemp→claude→tail→grep→shred block in a subshell with local EXIT trap that shreds $out_file on any exit path (normal return, signal, set -e). Subshell inherits parent INT/TERM traps but its own EXIT trap runs first, guaranteeing cleanup before propagation. Parent shell trap semantics unchanged.
outcome_evidence: bash -n syntax check passed; opt-worktree.sh committed and pushed auto/opt-scripts-shift-1782930634 to origin; direction-shift triggered automatically (autonomous-loop.sh listed in public-interfaces.txt); fix isolated in worktree branch, main untouched.
next_suggestion: Next pending fix: AS-EC-008 (priority 8) opt-worktree.sh cmd_commit orphaned files on commit failure (cd side effect). Route-fix in existing opt-scripts or opt-hooks worktree.
